title: MoEs Are Stronger than You Think: Hyper-Parallel Inference Scaling with RoE
authors: Not fully specified in provided text
abstract: This paper introduces hyper-parallel scaling, a novel inference paradigm that allocates additional computation at test time to diversify a model's internal computations, thereby improving the quality of each generated token. The authors propose Roster of Experts (RoE), a training-free inference technique for Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) models that adds controlled stochasticity to the router's expert selection, runs multiple stochastic forward passes per token, and aggregates the resulting logits into a single, higher-quality prediction.
key_contributions: Development of Roster of Experts (RoE), a training-free inference technique for MoE models
Implementation of specialized caching mechanism to reduce KV-cache size

methodology.approach: RoE adds controlled stochasticity to the router's expert selection, runs multiple stochastic forward passes per token, and aggregates the resulting logits into a single prediction
methodology.optimization: Exploits overlap across forward passes and merges them into a single batched call to the LLM, with specialized caching mechanism

comparison_with_existing_approaches.sequential_scaling: Examples include Chain-of-Thought (Wei et al., 2022) where the model produces longer, more structured outputs
comparison_with_existing_approaches.parallel_scaling: Examples include self-consistency (Wang et al., 2022) where multiple independent sequences are generated and then aggregated
comparison_with_existing_approaches.hyper_parallel_scaling: Novel paradigm that increases the model's internal compute during inference to enhance the quality of every generated token

evaluation.approach: Isolates gains attributable to hyper-parallel scaling by focusing on greedy decoding quality throughout the paper
